query: We just hit a 500 on the billing page when you click an invoice row. Can you file a GitHub issue for it?
note: Operator reports a bug and explicitly wants a GitHub issue filed.

query: Make a ticket on GitHub for the missing hover affordance on the status pills.
note: 'Make a ticket' is the casual phrasing the description calls out, scoped to GitHub.

query: Here's a stack trace from prod. Write that up so an agent can pick it up and ship a fix without asking us anything.
note: Pasted error plus the zero-follow-up groomed-issue bar — core use case, skill not named.

query: Triage the open issue queue before we dispatch — classify each stub as ready, needs-spec-input, or blocked.
note: Recon/intake-triage of stub issues into the three terminal states lives in this skill.

query: While fixing the auth bug I noticed the rate limiter is also broken. Don't fix it here — capture it as a follow-up.
note: Adjacent work mid-task becomes a follow-up issue instead of scope creep — explicit in the description.

query: This bug report covers three unrelated problems. Split it into separate GitHub issues with proper bodies.
note: Split a multi-theme report into separate filed issues — one theme per issue.

query: Draft a groomed issue body with the six sections and verified file:line refs for the export timeout.
note: Six-section groomed-issue format with verified evidence is exactly this skill.

query: The dropdown doesn't close on Escape. Can you write that up for the repo?
note: Casual bug report, 'write that up', GitHub repo implied — should file.

query: A feature request came in to add CSV export to the reports view. Turn it into trackable work on GitHub.
note: Feature request becoming a trackable GitHub issue is a named trigger.

query: Enrich issue #214 — it's too thin to dispatch. Add the acceptance criteria and affected-code section.
note: Enrich/triage an existing GitHub issue to the grooming bar.

query: We have a customer complaint about slow checkout. File a Jira ticket for it in the BILL project.
note: Cross-tracker confuser — filing, but the tracker is Jira, so the jira-* sibling fires.

query: Lock issue #312 before you dispatch an agent at it so nobody else double-claims it.
note: Claiming/locking a GitHub issue before dispatch belongs to gh-issue-locking, not filing.

query: Put agent-effort:high and pin agent-model:opus on issue #88 before we run it.
note: Applying model/effort control-plane labels is the gh-issue-labels skill.

query: This issue is vague — sit with the project lead and ask questions until the story reflects what they actually want.
note: Interactive grooming to assert intent (the WHAT/WHY) is grooming-issues, not the filing format.

query: Issue #150 is already groomed and accepted — do a deep technical recon and give me an LoE estimate before we size it.
note: Dev-side recon with implementation approach + LoE is technical-recon; not the cheap intake triage here.

query: Spawn an implementation sub-agent to actually build issue #99 and open the PR.
note: Dispatching an impl agent from a groomed issue is dispatching-subagents.

query: PR #441 is green but won't merge — figure out why and re-arm auto-merge.
note: Post-open PR-to-merge lifecycle, no issue authoring involved.

query: Add a new field to the BUG issue type in Jira and set its Priority to Highest.
note: Jira field/priority control plane — cross-tracker label/field confuser.

query: Our test suite is flaky on macOS runners — can you update the CI matrix to add Windows?
note: Direct implementation request (update CI config), not a request to file/triage an issue.

query: Summarize what changed in the last release and email it to the team.
note: Truly unrelated — release summary/email, no GitHub issue work.
